texts.full_text: beneath the pleura: report of a case. We report herein a rare case of a 21-year-old man with Rendu-Osler-Weber disease, otherwise known as hemorrhagic teleangiectasia, in whom a sponIntrapleural rupture of a pulmonary arteriovenous fistula occurring just beneath the pleura: report of a case. We report herein a rare case of a 21-year-old man with Rendu-Osler-Weber disease, otherwise known as hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia (HHT), in whom a spongelike malformation of blood vessels within the lung led to an intrapleural rupture. The patient presented with sudden onset of chest pain and dyspnea, which rapidly progressed over several hours. On physical examination, there were signs of pleural effusion on the right side. Chest X-ray revealed a large right-sided pleural effusion, and subsequent thoracentesis yielded blood-tinged fluid, confirming hemothorax. Computed tomography (CT) angiography demonstrated a pulmonary arteriovenous fistula located in the right lower lobe, just beneath the visceral pleura, with evidence of recent rupture into the pleural space. The patient was promptly taken to surgery for exploration and repair. Intraoperative findings confirmed the presence of an extensive spongelike vascular malformation that had ruptured through the thin layer of surrounding lung tissue into the pleural cavity. The fistula was successfully ligated, and the involved segment of the lung was resected. Postoperatively, the patient recovered well without complications. Follow-up imaging at 6 months showed no recurrence

texts.full_text: can I group by Time? I tried this, but it gives the error "Invalid column name 'Time'.": select Count(Page) as VisitingCount, CONVERT(VARCHAR(5), Date, 108) as [Time] from scr_SecuristLog where Date between '2009-05-04 00:00:00' and '2009-05-06 14:58' and [user] in (select USERNAME from scr_CustomerAuthorities) group by [Time] order by [VisitingCoYou cannot group by an alias, which is what `[Time]` is. You need to either use the entire expression in the `GROUP BY` clause or give it a name and then select from a derived table/subquery. Here are two ways you can modify your query: Method 1: Using the entire expression ```sql SELECT COUNT(Page) AS VisitingCount, CONVERT(VARCHAR(5), Date, 108) AS [Time] FROM scr_SecuristLog WHERE Date BETWEEN '2009-05-04 00:00:00' AND '2009-05-06 14:58' AND [user] IN (SELECT USERNAME FROM scr_CustomerAuthorities) GROUP BY CONVERT(VARCHAR(5), Date, 108) -- group by the entire expression ORDER BY VisitingCount; ``` Method 2: Using a derived table/subquery ```sql SELECT VisitingCount, [Time] FROM ( SELECT COUNT(Page) AS VisitingCount, CONVERT(VARCHAR(5), Date, 108) AS [Time] FROM scr_SecuristLog WHERE Date BETWEEN '2009-05-04 00:00:00' AND '2009-05-06 14:58' AND [user] IN (SELECT USERNAME FROM scr_CustomerAuthorities) ) AS Subquery GROUP BY [Time] ORDER BY VisitingCount; ``` Method 3: Using a Common Table Expression (CTE) ```sql WITH CTE AS ( SELECT Page, CONVERT(VARCHAR(5), Date, 108) AS [Time] FROM scr_SecuristLog WHERE Date BETWEEN '2009-05-04 00:00:00' AND '2009-05-06 14:58' AND [user] IN (SELECT USERNAME FROM scr_CustomerAuthorities)

texts.full_text: If you're traveling with kids, here's what we like to bring as gifts for the little ones (and their parents!): 1. Stuffed Animals - These are always a hit with both kids and adults in Costa Rica. 2. Candy - We usually take some sort of candy or small snacks that our kids can share with any local children they meet along the way. Gifts for volunteers: If you're traveling to volunteer, here's what we suggest bringing as gifts: 1. School Supplies – Pens, pencils, notebooks and anything else a school might need are always appreciated. 2. Clothing - Especially if it’s winter in Costa Rica (yes, that does happen!), warm clothing is very much needed. Gifts for locals: If you're looking to give something back to the local community or support small businesses, here's what we suggest: 1. First Aid Kits – These are always useful and can be a lifesaver. 2. Clothing - Especially if it’s winter in Costa Rica (yes, that does happen!), warm clothing is very much needed. Remember when choosing gifts to take with you on your travels, consider the cultural norms of the country you're visiting. It's also important to know what type of gift is appropriate for different occasions and relationshIps. During his teenage years he continued to assist his father with client projects. This early interest in design provided a good foundation for his undergraduate training at Pratt Institute in Brooklyn, New York. He has worked as an illustrator and graphic designer, creating business and brand identities as well. His archives are located at the Hagley Museum, Delaware, and other examples of his artwork are in the collection of Yeshiva University Museum.

They also mention that even though C can sometimes be close to these high-level proofs, it often pales compared to their clarity and expressiveness—highlighting why moving above such lower-tier languages is beneficial. The user suggests an interesting academic project: re-implementing parts of MirageOS's TLS in FStar. This could serve as a valuable exercise for exploring the boundaries between verification tools like F* (which can provide deep assurance through proof) and their translated implementations, potentially enhancing both our understanding and capabilities with formal methods. They’ve left their mark on our culture, and completely shifted customer expectations for what marketing and advertising can and should do. One such campaign that marketers are still talking about and learning from, more than 20 years on, is Apple’s 1997 campaign, “Think Different. The campaign, which featured black and white portraits and footage of iconic, “crazy” and “misfit” personalities, from Albert Einstein and Bob Dylan, to Muhammad Ali and John Lennon, is iconic. The campaign’s “Here’s to the crazy ones” manifesto, which is still featured and referenced to this day, is the stuff of marketing and advertising legend. The cause of thehypercalcaemia was localised to theright superior parathyroid glandwhich was subsequently removed. The patient's calcium homeostasis normalized postoperatively with no evidenceof recurrence at one year follow-up. The underlying mechanismcausing hypercalcaemia in this caseis discussed. texts.full_text: easy to master. But in decisions about how to teach Arabic in schools, beliefs about communication methods often trump research on the ways people learn information. Grade 1 Arabic curricula focus on discussing topics, such as the family or the environment, with verbs mainly presented in the present tense, first or third person. And no links are made between Standard Arabic and the vernacular grammars that students already know. Not surprisingly, research suggests that students perceive Standard Arabic as a foreign language. By the time they have sampled enough of its grammatical framework in class, years of poor comprehension and performance have passed. In the Maghreb, where the linguistic distance from Standard Arabic is considerable, many students drop out without learning much from these curricula. Ineffective Instruction Given these circumstances, one would think that Arabic curricula would devote much time to structuring the language, but the methods are somehow perverse. texts.full_text: ducted a population-based cohort study among 1036 women in San Francisco Bay Area who were aged 40 years or older when diagnosed with DCIS, treated by lumpectomy alone, and followed from January 1983 to December 1994. Factors associated with recurrence were identified using conditional logistic regression models. Overall, 7.6% of women experienced DCIS recurrence and 8.3% of women experienced invasive breast cancer recurrence during a median follow-up of 77.9 months. The risk of recurrence was higher among women who were younger at the age of diagnosis, had tumors with positive necrosis finding on pathology review, or had tumors <20 mm in size located <0.5 mm from the margin of excision. Clinical and pathological factors associated with recurrence have a wide range, suggesting that clinical management strategies should not be based on the assumption that all women are at the same risk of recurrence. BACKGROUND: This study assessed the association between body mass index (BMI) and breast cancer prognosis in an ethnically diverse cohort of women enrolled in a population-based study. METHODS: We conducted a multiethnic population-based case-control study of incident, invasive breast cancer cases diagnosed from 1984 through 1994 among women 20-74 years of age residing in the San Francisco Bay Area. RESULTS: Among 1546 breast cancer patients who survived at least 5 months after diagnosis, there were significant associations between BMI and prognosis (both disease-free and overall survival) among premenopausal cases, with increasing risks associated with higher BMI levels. The multivariable hazard ratios comparing highest to lowest quartiles of BMI (<22 versus 27+) kg/m(2) were 1.65 (95% confidence interval [CI] = 1.08 to 2.53), P(trend) = 0.02 and 1.94 (95% CI = 1.17 to 3.19), P(trend) = 0.05, respectively. texts.full_text: company's services focus on online brand protection and anticounterfeiting. MarkMonitor's domain management service "ensures domains are safe with a 'hardened' portal and a full suite of premium security solutions, including advanced security measures at the registrar level -- and, security services to lock domains down to the registry level," the company's website says. It seems that SEA targeted MarkMonitor in order to attack Facebook in particular as the company celebrated its 10th anniversary Tuesday. The group used the MarkMonitor control panel to modify the WHOIS information for facebook.com, changing the domain's contact address to Damscus, Syria. The hackers failed to modify the domain's DNS (domain name system) settings and point the website to a server under their control, as they did in the past with the domain names of other companies. That's because facebook.com has a registry lock in place, a feature that requires additional human-based verification at the registry level for making changes to a domain name. The registry for the .com TLD zone is VeriSign. It's not clear how SEA obtained access to the MarkMonitor control panel, but from other screen shots published by the hackers, the panel also gave them access to the domain names of Amazon, Google, Yahoo and many other well-known companies from different industries. Domain whois queries for amazon.com, google.com and yahoo.com all show MarkMonitor as the registrar, but like facebook.com, all of those domain name have the "clientUpdateProhibited" flag which indicates the presence of a registry lock. This means SEA wouldn't have been able to hijack those domain names either. MarkMonitor, which is owned by Thomson Reuters, did not immediately respond to an inquiry seeking more information about the attack. Facebook declined to comment, but its domain's whois information was quickly corrected following the incident. texts.full_text: urs following PGE2 addition. Ide: These findings suggest that eicosanoids play a significant role in modulating PDGF-R activity within human lung fibroblasts culture systems. Further research into the interaction between these two biological paThways could potentially lead to new therapeutic strategies for controlling cell proliferation and other processes associated with respiratory diseases or disorders involving excessive tissue growth, such as asthma, emphysema, bronchitis, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), lung cancer metastasis, fibrosis among others. Understanding the molecular mechanisms underlying these interactions may also help in developing targeted treatments that could enhance cell signaling regulation and improve patient outcomes by preventing or mitigating undesirable side effects of current therapies which often have non-specific targets due to their broad spectrum activity against multiple pathways simultaneously being activated during pathological conditions like inflammation, tissue repair etc. Additionally studying how eicosanoids influence other growth factor receptors might broaden our understanding about the complex network controlling cell behavior in health and disease states providing insights into potential therapeutic applications beyond just PDGF-R targeting

texts.full_text: toxocariasis is ELISA using excretory - secretory antigens of third stage larvae (ES/L3). These antigens are glycoproteins, which originate in the secretory organs of the parasite and are non species-specific. Sera from patients with other helminthiases and non- parasitic diseases were used to evaluate the specificity of ELISA using the excretory - secretory antigen (ES/L3). The reactivity of these sera was between 11 and 70%. Western blot using patients' sera revealed that the glycoprotein triplet having a molecular weight of 120 kDa was responsible for cross-reactivity. With these results, and for the purpose of purifying the antigen, ion exchange chromatography was performed. When the sera from patients with various parparasitic diseases and non-parasitic conditions were tested against the purified antigen fractions obtained through ion exchange chromatography. The purified antigen demonstrated significantly reduced cross-reactivity compared to the crude ES/L3 antigen, with reactivity rates dropping to 3–15% in sera from patients with helminthiases such as spp. or infections. Western blot analysis of the purified antigen revealed the absence of the 120 kDa glycoprotein triplet responsible for cross-reactivity, confirming the successful removal of contaminating epitopes. Further validation using ELISA showed that the purified antigen retained high sensitivity (92–95%) for toxocariasis while achieving improved specificity (88–91%) in distinguishing it from other parasitic and non-parasitic conditions. texts.full_text: the person with whom I was talking would lean in towards me and say "Do you reaLly think America is ready for a black/woman president?" Like many people, I found the former question racist and the latter one sexist. As a white man, I also resented the implication that I somehow understood that America wasn't ready, whatever that meant. Initially, I responded to these questions with anger, but then began to preempt them. Whenever the presidential election came up in discussion, which was about ten times a day, I began the conversation by leaning forward and asking "Do you really think America is ready for another white male president?" This usually caught people off guard and often led to more productive conversations about what it means to be 'ready' for someone based on their race or gender. However, one day at work, I decided to try something different. When a colleague asked me if the country was prepared for its first female commander-in-chief, instead of responding with my usual question, I simply said "Yes." The colleague looked surprised and then asked why I thought so. This gave me an opportunity to talk about Hillary Clinton's qualifications, her experience as a senator from New York, and the fact that she had won more votes than any other presidential candidate in history at that point. This approach seemed to have a bigger impact on people because it focused not just on their biases but also highlighted the strengths of those
